title: euclea racemosa subsp. racemosa
intro: Euclea racemosa subsp. racemosa, also known as the Blue Guarri or Buffalo Horn, is a plant species in the family Ebenaceae, native to southern Africa. Traditionally, the roots and bark of Euclea racemosa subsp. racemosa have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Euclea racemosa subsp. racemosa have been found to have potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-diarrheal effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots and bark of Euclea racemosa subsp. racemosa can be made into a tea, decoction, or infusion, and are also used in tinctures and capsules for medicinal purposes.
